item.ptxt: When DTC P0300 / P0301 / P0302 / P0303 / P0304 is detected, MIL blinks or lights up according to detecting condition. For details, refer to DTC P0300 / P0301 / P0302 / P0303 / P0304 .
With the CAN communication generic scan tool, only asterisk (*) marked DTC in the following table can be read.
For “3 D/C / 1 D/C” (DTC detection logic) with asterisk (*) in this table, its operation is different depending on detecting condition.
For details of operation, refer to each “DTC Detecting Condition and Trouble Area”.
For “Light up / off” (MIL operation) with asterisk (*) in this table, its operation is different depending on detecting condition.
For details of operation, refer to each “DTC Detecting Condition and Trouble Area”.

entry.ptxt: Intake OCV control circuit is open.
“A” Camshaft Position Timing Over Advanced or System Performance
Measured intake CMP does not reach target intake CMP for specified time.
Exhaust OCV control circuit is open.
“B” Camshaft Position Timing Over Advanced or System Performance
Measured exhaust CMP does not reach target exhaust CMP for specified time.
A/F sensor heater control circuit is open.
A/F sensor heater control circuit is shorted to ground.
A/F sensor heater control circuit is shorted to power supply.
HO2S heater control circuit is open.
HO2S heater control circuit is shorted to ground.
HO2S heater control circuit is shorted to power supply.
Mass or Volume Air Flow “A” Circuit
Output voltage of MAF sensor signal circuit is higher than 0.5 V, even if MAF sensor power supply is shut-off.
Mass or Volume Air Flow “A” Circuit Range / Performance
Measured MAF sensor signal is out of specified range for 5 sec.
Mass or Volume Air Flow “A” Circuit Low
Output voltage of MAF sensor signal circuit is lower than 0.02 V for 5 sec.
Mass or Volume Air Flow “A” Circuit High
Output voltage of MAF sensor signal circuit is higher than 4.74 V for 5 sec.
Manifold Absolute Pressure / Barometric Pressure Circuit Range / Performance
Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Difference between measured and estimated MAP is out of specification for 5 sec.
MAP value at engine start is smaller than barometric pressure sensor value by more than specified value.

entry.ptxt: Manifold Absolute Pressure / Barometric Pressure Circuit Low
Output voltage of MAP sensor signal circuit is lower than 0.01 V for 5 sec.
Manifold Absolute Pressure / Barometric Pressure Circuit High
Output voltage of MAP sensor signal circuit is higher than 4.74 V for 5 sec.
Intake Air Temperature Sensor 1 Circuit Low
Output voltage of IAT sensor signal circuit is lower than 0.01 V for 5 sec.
Intake Air Temperature Sensor 1 Circuit High
Output voltage of IAT sensor signal circuit is higher than 4.86 V for 5 sec.
Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor 1 Circuit Range / Performance
Difference between measured ECT and estimated ECT is more than specified value for specified time with engine running.
Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor 1 Circuit Low
Output voltage of ECT sensor signal circuit is lower than 0.01 V for 2 sec.
Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor 1 Circuit High
Output voltage of ECT sensor signal circuit is higher than 4.86 V for 2 sec.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “A” Circuit Low
Output voltage of TP sensor (main) signal circuit is lower than 0.2 V.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “A” Circuit High
Output voltage of TP sensor (main) signal circuit is higher than 4.8 V.
Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Output voltage of A/F sensor signal (VM) circuit is lower than specified value for 10 sec. with engine running.
Output voltage of A/F sensor signal (UN) circuit is lower than specified value for 10 sec. with engine running.
Output voltage of A/F sensor signal (IA) circuit is lower than specified value for 10 sec. with engine running.

entry.ptxt: Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Output voltage of A/F sensor signal (VM) circuit is higher than specified value for 10 sec. with engine running.
Output voltage of A/F sensor signal (UN) circuit is higher than specified value for 10 sec. with engine running.
Output voltage of A/F sensor signal (IA) circuit is higher than specified value for 10 sec. with engine running.

entry.ptxt: Shifting time between rich and lean of A/F sensor is longer than specified time.
Temperature of A/F sensor element is lower than specified value for 60 sec. even when A/F sensor heater control duty ratio is higher than specified value.
When HO2S signal voltage is lower than 0.06 V, HO2S signal circuit voltage is not change for 5 sec with applying voltage to HO2S signal circuit.
Output voltage of HO2S signal circuit is higher than 1.1 V for 5 sec.
O2 Sensor Circuit No Activity Detected (Sensor 2)
Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: HO2S signal circuit is open.
Internal resistance of HO2S is higher than specification, even if estimated exhaust gas temperature is higher than specification.

entry.ptxt: Fuel trim correction is higher than specified value for specified time under specified vehicle condition.
Fuel trim correction is lower than specified value for specified time under specified vehicle condition.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “B” Circuit Low
Output voltage of TP sensor (sub) signal circuit is lower than 0.20 V.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “B” Circuit High
Output voltage of TP sensor (sub) signal circuit is higher than 4.81 V.
Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Misfire, which causes catalyst to overheat during 200 engine revolutions, is detected at 2 or more cylinders. (MIL blinks as long as misfire lasts.)
Misfire, which affects exhaust emission adversely during 1000 engine revolutions, is detected at 2 or more cylinders.

entry.ptxt: Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Misfire, which causes catalyst to overheat during 200 engine revolutions, is detected at specific cylinder. (MIL blinks as long as misfire lasts.)
Misfire, which affects exhaust emission adversely during 1000 engine revolutions, is detected at specific cylinder.

entry.ptxt: Output voltage of knock sensor signal circuit is lower than specified value for specified time with engine running.
Output voltage of knock sensor signal circuit is higher than specified value for specified time with engine running.
CKP sensor signal is not inputted while CMP sensor signal is being inputted.
Crankshaft Position Sensor “A” Circuit Range / Performance
Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Signal detecting time is longer than specification.
Number of teeth between gaps is more than specification.
Time between adjacent two teeth is longer than specification.

entry.ptxt: Any of the following error counters is more than specified value with engine running.

item.ptxt: Intake CMP sensor signal is not inputted while CKP sensor signal is being inputted.
Intake CMP sensor signal is more than specified counters when output signal of CKP sensor is normal.

entry.ptxt: Any of the following error counters is more than specified value with engine running.

item.ptxt: Exhaust CMP sensor signal is not inputted while CKP sensor signal is being inputted.
Exhaust CMP sensor signal is more than specified counters when output signal of CKP sensor is normal.

entry.ptxt: The calculated degradation index is below specified value.
Evaporative Emission System Purge Control Valve Circuit
EVAP canister purge valve control circuit is open with engine running.
Evaporative Emission System Purge Control Valve Circuit Low
EVAP canister purge valve control circuit is shorted to ground with engine running.
Evaporative Emission System Purge Control Valve Circuit High
EVAP canister purge valve control circuit is shorted to power supply with engine running.
Radiator cooling fan relay No.1 drive circuit is shorted to ground or open.
Radiator cooling fan relay No.2 and/or No.3 drive circuit is shorted to ground or open.
Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Vehicle speed is slower than 4 km/h (2.5 mile/h) for 4 sec. at fuel cut.
Vehicle speed is not input by CAN communication.

entry.ptxt: Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Both brake light switch signal voltage and brake switch signal voltage are at low level for specified time.
Both brake light switch signal voltage and brake switch signal voltage are at high level for specified time.

entry.ptxt: Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Output voltage of battery temperature signal circuit is lower than 0.1 V for 5 sec.
Output voltage of battery temperature signal circuit is higher than 4.8 V for 5 sec.

entry.ptxt: Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Output voltage of A/C refrigerant pressure sensor signal circuit is lower than 0.15 V.
Output voltage of A/C refrigerant pressure sensor signal circuit is higher than 4.93 V.

entry.ptxt: Main power supply voltage is lower than 2.54 V for 10 sec.
Main power supply circuit voltage is lower than 9.8 V for 10 sec. with engine running.
Main power supply circuit voltage is higher than 18 V for 10 sec. with engine running.
Internal failure in ECM is detected.
Battery voltage stays lower than 12.3 V for longer than 30 sec. even with C duty ratio more than 65% and FR duty ratio less than 60%.
FR duty ratio stays 0% for longer than 5 sec., while C duty ratio being more than 50%.
Fuel pump relay drive circuit is open.
Fuel pump relay drive circuit is shorted to ground.
Fuel pump relay drive circuit is shorted to power supply.
A/C compressor relay drive circuit is open.
A/C Clutch Relay Control Circuit Low
A/C compressor relay drive circuit is shorted to ground.
A/C Clutch Relay Control Circuit High
A/C compressor relay drive circuit is shorted to power supply.
Radiator cooling fan relay No.1 drive circuit is shorted to power supply.
Radiator cooling fan relay No.2 and/or No.3 drive circuit is shorted to power supply.
Generator Current Sensor Circuit Range / Performance
Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Output voltage of electric load current sensor signal circuit is more than 4.8 V for 5 sec.
Output voltage of electric load current sensor signal circuit is lower than 0.2 V for 5 sec.
Output voltage of electric load current sensor signal circuit fluctuates less than specified value for 5 sec.

entry.ptxt: Immobilizer ID and/or Password Not Registered

item.ptxt: Ignition switch model:
Keyless push start model:

entry.ptxt: “A” Camshaft Position Actuator Control Circuit Low
Intake OCV control circuit is shorted to ground.
“A” Camshaft Position Actuator Control Circuit High
Intake OCV control circuit is shorted to power supply.
“B” Camshaft Position Actuator Control Circuit Low
Exhaust OCV control circuit is shorted to ground.
“B” Camshaft Position Actuator Control Circuit High
Exhaust OCV control circuit is shorted to power supply.
Throttle Actuator “A” Control Motor Circuit / Open
Throttle actuator and/or its circuit malfunction (open, short to power supply or short to ground) is detected.
Throttle Actuator “A” Control Motor Circuit Range / Performance
Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Difference between measured throttle valve position and targeted throttle valve position is more than specified value.
Duty ratio of throttle actuator signal is out of specified range for specified time.

entry.ptxt: Throttle Actuator Control Throttle Body Range / Performance
Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Time taken by throttle valve to return from partially open position to mechanical default position is more than specified time during return spring check process after setting ignition “OFF”.
Measured default throttle position is out of specified range.

entry.ptxt: Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “D” Circuit Low
Output voltage of APP sensor (main) signal circuit is lower than 0.7 V.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “D” Circuit High
Output voltage of APP sensor (main) signal circuit is higher than 4.8 V.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “E” Circuit Low
Output voltage of APP sensor (sub) signal circuit is lower than 0.3 V.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “E” Circuit High
Output voltage of APP sensor (sub) signal circuit is higher than 2.5 V.
Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “A”/”B” Voltage Correlation
Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Difference between measured throttle valve position by TP sensor (main) and estimated throttle valve position is more than specified value.
Difference between measured throttle valve position by TP sensor (sub) and estimated throttle valve position is more than specified value.

entry.ptxt: Throttle / Pedal Position Sensor / Switch “D”/”E” Voltage Correlation
Difference between half of APP (main) sensor signal and APP (sub) sensor signal is more than specified value.
Throttle Actuator Control System - Idle Position Not Learned
Electric throttle control system calibration is not completed or calibrated throttle position is out of specified range.
Output voltage of barometric pressure sensor signal is lower than 0.2 V for 5 sec.
Output voltage of barometric pressure sensor signal is higher than 4.8 V for 5 sec.
O2 Sensor Signal Circuit Shorted to Heater Circuit (Sensor 1)
Variation of A/F sensor current is higher than specified value with engine running.
O2 Sensor Positive Current Control Circuit / Open (Sensor 1)
Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: A/F sensor signal stays within specified value for specified time.
Output voltage of A/F sensor signal (IP) circuit drops to lower than 1.7 V for 5 sec. during fuel-cut control.
Output voltage of A/F sensor signal (IP) circuit remains within specified range for 10 sec. even during enrichment / enleanment control.

entry.ptxt: O2 Sensor Reference Voltage Circuit / Open (Sensor 1)
Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Output voltage of A/F sensor signal (UN) circuit is higher than 4.7 V for 5 sec. with engine running.
Output voltage of A/F sensor signal (UN) circuit is lower than 0.3 V for 5 sec. with engine running.

entry.ptxt: O2 Sensor Negative Current Control Circuit / Open (Sensor 1)
Output voltage of A/F sensor signal (VM) circuit is 1.47 to 1.53 V for 5 sec.
O2 Sensor Signal Biased / Stuck Lean (Sensor 2)
HO2S signal stays in lean state for 100 sec. even when A/F enrichment control.
O2 Sensor Signal Biased / Stuck Rich (Sensor 2)
Any of the following conditions is met.

item.ptxt: Output voltage of HO2S signal circuit is higher than 0.15 V for specified time after warm-up even when vehicle is in fuel cut driving condition.
HO2S signal stays in rich state for 100 sec. even during A/F enleanment control.

entry.ptxt: O2 Sensor Pumping Current Trim Circuit / Open (Sensor 1)
Output voltage of A/F sensor signal (IA) circuit is higher than 4.8 V for 5 sec. with engine running.
Refer to CAN DTC (Lost Communication and Communication Bus Off) Table .
Lost Communication With ESP® Control Module
Lost Communication With Power Steering Control Module
Lost Communication With Body Control Module
Lost Communication With Body Control Module “keyless start”
Lost Communication With Instrument Panel Cluster (IPC) Control Module
